target.name: Lightelligence
target.founder: Shen Yichen, Marin Soljačić, Huaiyu Meng, Spencer Powers

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $23B

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Lightelligence is a public-stage semiconductor company that has raised $270M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Cerebras ($23.0B), Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), Ouster ($1.5B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given that Lightelligence's revenue remains undisclosed, precluding a public-comp anchoring analysis using the semiconductor sector's 25.3x P/S median. The peer-median valuation across the four public-stage chip companies yields $3.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $23.0B. This method reflects comparable companies at an identical stage of maturity and sector focus.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $3.7B (median: $3.2B), derived from a single methodological anchor. The wide dispersion in the peer set—a 23x gap between minimum and maximum—reflects material heterogeneity in the semiconductor subsector and stage-specific volatility. Key uncertainties include Lightelligence's revenue trajectory, gross margins, and addressable market position relative to peers. The reliance on one applicable method limits conviction; additional clarity on revenue run rate and customer concentration would strengthen the valuation framework.
